articles.title: Daily Piano Focus: 20 Minutes a Day for Young Beginners
articles.summary: A simple 20-minute-a-day piano practice routine designed for kids ages 6–10 that keeps practice fun, focused, and consistent.
articles.content:
For young beginners, consistency matters far more than marathon practice sessions. A focused 20 minutes a day at the piano can build strong habits without overwhelming your child.

Here is a sample 20-minute routine for a 7-year-old beginner:

Minutes 0–3: Warm-up
- Sit with good posture and curved fingers.
- Play simple five-finger patterns in C position.

Minutes 3–8: Review pieces
- Play 1–2 familiar songs assigned by the teacher.
- Aim for smooth transitions and steady rhythm.

Minutes 8–15: New assignment
- Slowly practice today’s new measure or line.
- Clap the rhythm first, then play hands separately.

Minutes 15–18: Rhythm or flashcard game
- Use note-naming cards or a rhythm-clap game.
- Keep it playful and short.

Minutes 18–20: Favorite piece
- End with a piece your child loves.
- Praise effort, not perfection.

Tips for parents:
- Keep the piano area free of distractions (no TV or tablet nearby).
- Encourage practice at the same time every day, such as after school.
- Use a simple calendar to check off each 20-minute session.

Over weeks and months, these focused 20-minute sessions help kids read music more confidently, develop good technique, and associate piano with success rather than stress.

articles.title: Piano Practice Rituals for Busy Adults
articles.summary: Learn how adults with full schedules can build effective 25–30 minute piano practice rituals that actually stick.
articles.content:
Adult piano students often struggle more with time than with technique. Instead of aiming for long sessions that rarely happen, build a realistic ritual you can repeat most days.

1. Define your minimum
Decide on a minimum commitment (for example, 25 minutes, four days a week). Protect this time like any other appointment.

2. Use a three-part structure
- 5 minutes: Technique (scales, chords, or a technical exercise)
- 15 minutes: Current repertoire (new pieces or sections)
- 5 minutes: Review or improvisation (play something you enjoy)

3. Remove friction
Keep your sheet music organized, your metronome handy, and your bench at the correct height so you can start playing immediately.

4. Track progress
Keep a small notebook by the piano. Write the date, what you practiced, and one small win.

5. Plan tomorrow’s focus
End each session by writing a one-line goal for the next practice: “Tomorrow: right hand of measures 9–12 at 60 bpm.”

These small structures help busy adults make steady progress even with limited time.

articles.title: 20 Minutes a Day: Essential Guitar Warm-Ups
articles.summary: Use a 20-minute daily warm-up routine to build strength, speed, and accuracy on guitar without over-practicing.
articles.content:
A focused 20-minute warm-up can transform your guitar playing over time. Instead of jumping straight into songs, use this structured approach:

Minutes 0–5: Finger independence
- Chromatic exercises on all six strings.
- Use a metronome at a comfortable tempo.

Minutes 5–10: Scales
- Choose 2–3 scales (for example, C major, G major, A minor).
- Play two octaves ascending and descending.

Minutes 10–15: Chord transitions
- Practice smooth transitions between common chord pairs.
- Aim for clean tone and minimal finger movement.

Minutes 15–20: Short lick or riff
- Work on a short riff or lick that challenges your current level.

Repeat this structure daily and gradually increase your metronome speed as it becomes comfortable.

articles.title: Teen Voice Practice: Building Strength Without Strain
articles.summary: A safe, 30-minute voice practice structure for teens that protects the changing voice while building solid technique.
articles.content:
Teen singers need thoughtful practice routines that protect the voice as it matures.

Warm-up (5–7 minutes):
- Gentle sirens and lip trills.
- Light humming on a comfortable range.

Technique (10 minutes):
- Simple vowel exercises on 5-note scales.
- Focus on breath support and relaxed jaw.

Repertoire (10–12 minutes):
- Work small sections of a song instead of full run-throughs.
- Alternate between chest and head voice sections.

Cool-down (3–5 minutes):
- Soothing humming slides downward.
- Gentle stretches of neck and shoulders.

Teens should stop immediately if they feel pain, tightness, or hoarseness, and discuss it with their teacher.

articles.title: Making the Most of Wednesday Drum Lessons
articles.summary: How to structure your week around a midweek drum lesson so practice feels natural, not forced.
articles.content:
A midweek drum lesson is perfectly positioned to divide your practice week into two short cycles. Here is a simple structure:

After your Wednesday lesson:
- Same day: 10–15 minutes reviewing new grooves.

Thursday–Friday:
- 20–25 minutes focusing on the hardest pattern from the lesson.
- Use a metronome and keep notes of your top tempo.

Weekend:
- One longer session (30–40 minutes) where you play along with songs.

Tuesday:
- Short review of difficult fills so they are fresh before your next Wednesday lesson.

This schedule breaks practice into manageable chunks and prevents the “cram the day before the lesson” habit.

articles.title: Saturday Focus Sessions for Intermediate Violinists
articles.summary: Design a powerful weekend violin practice session that supports your weekly lessons.
articles.content:
Intermediate violinists benefit from one longer, highly focused practice block each week.

Plan a 45–60 minute Saturday session:

1. Warm-up and intonation (10–15 minutes)
- Long bows on open strings.
- Slow scales with a tuner or drone.

2. Etudes or exercises (10–15 minutes)
- Choose 1–2 etudes that target current technical goals.

3. Repertoire (20–25 minutes)
- Divide your piece into small sections.
- Rotate focus each Saturday so every section gets deep work.

4. Musicality (5–10 minutes)
- Experiment with dynamics, phrasing, and vibrato.

This Saturday routine, combined with shorter weekday practice, creates a balanced schedule that supports steady progress.

articles.title: Our East Branch Studio Expands Group Voice Offerings
articles.summary: Starting Spring 2025, the East Branch Studio will offer more teen group voice classes on weekday evenings.
articles.content:
We are excited to announce an expansion of our teen group voice program at the East Branch Studio.

Beginning March 2025, we will:
- Add new Tuesday and Thursday evening group voice sections for ages 13–17.
- Keep class sizes small (6–8 students) for individual attention.
- Align curriculum with our recital and performance calendar.

Current and new families can view updated schedules and pricing on our Lessons page under Group Classes → Voice → Teens (13–17).

articles.title: Spring 2025 Beginner Piano Recitals: What to Expect
articles.summary: A guide for families participating in our free May 2025 beginner piano recitals, including how to prepare a 9-year-old student.
articles.content:
Our Spring 2025 beginner piano recitals are designed to be welcoming and low-pressure, especially for students under age 10.

Key details:
- Recitals take place throughout May 2025 at our Main and East Branch Studios.
- Events are free for current students and their families.
- Each student typically performs one short piece (30–90 seconds).

How to prepare a 9-year-old beginner:
- Start daily run-throughs of the recital piece 3–4 weeks before the event.
- Encourage slow, confident playing rather than speed.
- Practice walking on stage, bowing, and sitting at the piano calmly.

Families can register through the Events & Recitals page by selecting the appropriate beginner piano recital date.

articles.title: Understanding Our Lesson Membership Plans
articles.summary: An overview of how our monthly lesson memberships work, including weekly 60-minute options for adult guitar and voice.
articles.content:
Our lesson membership plans are designed to make billing predictable and attendance consistent.

Key points:
- Memberships include a set number of weekly lessons per month (usually 4).
- Plans are organized by instrument, age group, and lesson length.
- Adult students can choose between 30-, 45-, and 60-minute weekly lessons.

For example, adult guitarists and vocalists often choose our 60-minute weekly private lesson plans for deeper work each session. You can compare prices on the Pricing page using the instrument and age filters, then click a plan to see what is included before starting enrollment.

articles.title: How to Handle Cancellations and Make-Up Lessons Smoothly
articles.summary: Tips for families on using our scheduling tools, understanding late cancellation rules, and booking make-up lessons.
articles.content:
Life happens, and sometimes you need to miss a lesson. To keep things fair for teachers and students, we follow clear cancellation and make-up policies.

General guidelines:
- Cancel or reschedule at least 24 hours in advance whenever possible.
- Use your online account or contact the front desk by phone or email.

Late cancellations:
- Cancellations made less than 24 hours before the lesson may be charged according to our Late Cancellation Policy, detailed on the FAQ & Policies page.

Make-up lessons:
- When available, make-up lessons can be scheduled directly through the Schedule page.
- Some make-ups may have a small fee; see the Make-up Lessons section of the FAQ for current rates.

Following these steps helps us keep schedules predictable and teachers compensated for their time.

articles.title: Meet Sophia Lee: Saturday Violin Specialist at the Main Studio
articles.summary: Get to know Sophia Lee, one of our most experienced Saturday violin teachers at the Main Studio.
articles.content:
Sophia Lee has over 10 years of experience teaching violin to students of all ages, with a special focus on intermediate and advanced players.

Sophia teaches primarily at our Main Studio on Saturdays and select weekday evenings. Her students describe her as patient, detail-oriented, and inspiring.

In lessons, Sophia emphasizes:
- Solid foundational technique.
- Musical expression and phrasing.
- Smart practice strategies for busy students.

You can view Sophia’s full bio, ratings, and schedule on the Teachers page by filtering for Violin at the Main Studio and selecting Saturday availability.

articles.title: Scheduling Siblings: Piano and Drums on the Same Afternoon
articles.summary: Strategies for booking piano and drum lessons for siblings on the same day with minimal waiting time.
articles.content:
When siblings study different instruments, careful scheduling can save families a lot of time.

Here are tips that work well at our Main Studio:

1. Start with the younger sibling’s lesson
Begin by choosing a 30-minute slot for the younger child (often piano). Aim for a start time shortly after school.

2. Add the older sibling’s longer lesson
Look for a 45- or 60-minute slot for the older sibling (for example, drums) that starts within 60 minutes of the first lesson ending.

3. Use the Schedule page filters
Filter by location (Main Studio), then set the day (Wednesday) and instrument, switching between piano and drums as needed.

4. Confirm both lessons in a combined booking summary
Our system will show both lessons together before you proceed to the details step, so you can double-check timing.

With a little planning, siblings can take back-to-back lessons with minimal downtime.

faq_items.question: What is your cancellation policy?
faq_items.answer_html:
We understand that schedules can change. To keep things fair for both families and teachers, we ask that you provide at least 24 hours' notice for any lesson you need to cancel or reschedule.

  • More than 24 hours' notice: You may cancel or reschedule without a fee. When possible, you may be offered a make-up option based on teacher availability.
  • Exactly 24 hours' notice: We treat this as an on-time cancellation and will do our best to help you reschedule.

Late cancellations (less than 24 hours)

Cancellations made less than 24 hours before your scheduled lesson are considered late cancellations:

  • The lesson is charged in full and is not eligible for a complimentary make-up.
  • In some cases, you may request a paid make-up lesson if your teacher has open time. See the FAQ item titled "Is there a fee for make-up lessons?" for current rates.
  • Repeated late cancellations may result in loss of your reserved weekly time slot.

To cancel or reschedule, please use your online account, call the front desk, or email us. The timestamp of your request determines whether it is considered on-time or late.

faq_items.question: How do I reschedule a lesson?
faq_items.answer_html:
You can reschedule a lesson in three ways:

  1. Log in to your online account, open the Schedule tab, select the lesson, and click Reschedule.
  2. Call the front desk during business hours and request a new time.
  3. Email your preferred dates and times to our scheduling team.

Reschedule requests must be received at least 24 hours before the original lesson start time to avoid being treated as a late cancellation. For short-notice changes, please review our "What is your cancellation policy?" FAQ item.

faq_items.question: What happens if I arrive late to my lesson?
faq_items.answer_html:
If you arrive late, your lesson will still end at the originally scheduled time so that we can stay on schedule for all students.

  • Up to 15 minutes late: The remaining portion of your lesson will be used as effectively as possible.
  • More than 15 minutes late: Your teacher may be marked as "student did not show". The lesson will be charged in full and is not eligible for a make-up.

If you know you will be late, please call the studio so we can alert your teacher. In some cases, a brief virtual (online) check-in can be arranged for the remaining time.

faq_items.question: Is there a fee for make-up lessons?
faq_items.answer_html:
In most cases, yes. Because make-up lessons require extra scheduling and often take place outside a teacher's regular teaching block, we charge a small fee to cover the instructor's time.

  • 30-minute make-up lesson: The standard make-up lesson fee is $15 per 30-minute make-up lesson.
  • 45-minute or 60-minute make-up lesson: The standard fee is $25 per make-up lesson for these longer durations.

Make-up lesson fees are charged to the primary payment method on file at the time the make-up is confirmed. Fees may be waived at the studio's discretion in rare circumstances (for example, severe weather closures initiated by the studio).

Please note: Late cancellations (less than 24 hours' notice) are not automatically eligible for make-up lessons. See our "What is your cancellation policy?" FAQ item for details.

faq_items.question: How do I schedule a make-up lesson?
faq_items.answer_html:
If you cancel on time and your lesson is eligible for a make-up, you will receive an email with a link to available make-up slots.

  1. Click the Schedule Make-Up link in the email or visit the Schedule page while logged in.
  2. Filter by instrument, location, and teacher (optional).
  3. Select an available make-up time that works for you.
  4. Review the displayed make-up lesson fee and click Confirm.

Make-up lessons are offered on a space-available basis and cannot be guaranteed with the same teacher or day of the week as your regular lesson. Make-up credits typically expire 30 days after the original missed lesson.

faq_items.question: How does membership billing work?
faq_items.answer_html:
Lesson memberships are billed on a monthly basis and include a set number of weekly lessons (usually 4) based on your plan.

  • Monthly tuition is charged to your card on file on the same calendar day each month as your original sign-up date.
  • Memberships cover your reserved weekly time slot; missed lessons are still charged according to our cancellation and make-up policies.
  • Any applicable signup fee will be charged once, at the time of enrollment.

For a summary of how to pause or cancel a membership, see the policy titled "Can I cancel my membership?" in the Studio Policies section.

faq_items.question: Do you offer trial lessons?
faq_items.answer_html:
Yes. Most private lesson offerings include the option to book a discounted trial lesson so you can meet the teacher and experience our studio before committing to a membership.

  • Look for the "Book Trial Lesson" button on the lesson detail page.
  • Trials are typically 30 minutes for younger students and may be 45 or 60 minutes for adults.
  • Trial lesson fees vary by instrument and location but are clearly displayed before you confirm.

After your trial, our team will help you choose a regular weekly time or membership plan if you wish to continue.

faq_items.question: What is the difference between online and in-studio lessons?
faq_items.answer_html:
We offer both online and in-studio lessons so you can choose the format that fits your schedule and learning style.

  • In-studio lessons: Take place at one of our physical studio locations, with access to acoustic instruments, sound-treated rooms, and in-person interaction with your teacher.
  • Online lessons: Take place over secure video conference from your home. These are great for busy adults, students with transportation challenges, or mild illness.

Curriculum, practice expectations, and teacher qualifications are consistent across both formats. Pricing may differ slightly; see the Lessons and Pricing pages for current rates for online vs in-studio offerings.

faq_items.question: Can I cancel my membership?
faq_items.answer_html:
Yes. You may cancel your lesson membership with 30 days' written notice.

  • All cancellation requests must be submitted by email so we have a written record.
  • Your membership will remain active, and lessons will continue, through the end of the current 30-day period.
  • No additional signup fees will be charged if you re-enroll within 6 months, but tuition rates are subject to change.

Membership cancellations do not waive any late cancellation charges or make-up lesson fees already incurred. Please review the cancellation and make-up lesson FAQ items for those details.

teachers.teaching_style: Patient and methodical, using clear goals and demonstrations to build confidence.

teachers.bio: Emily specializes in beginner and early-intermediate piano students and loves helping children build strong, positive practice habits.
teachers.education: BA in Music Education, Berklee College of Music
teachers.teaching_style: Warm and structured, using games and clear routines to make practice feel manageable.
teachers.availability_summary: Teaches kids and teen piano at Main Studio Monday–Thursday afternoons and early evenings; online slots available on Wednesdays.

teachers.bio: Marcus is a drummer and band coach who helps students connect technique with real-world music-making.
teachers.education: Diploma in Contemporary Performance, The Collective School of Music
teachers.teaching_style: Energetic and practical, focusing on grooves, time feel, and playing with tracks.
teachers.availability_summary: Leads teen drum lessons at Main Studio on Wednesday and Friday afternoons, with additional Saturday slots and online options.

teachers.bio: Jordan teaches guitar to teens and adults, from first chords to advanced lead playing. He also coaches students in songwriting.
teachers.education: BM in Guitar Performance, University of Miami
teachers.teaching_style: Goal-focused and flexible, blending technique with students' favorite styles.
teachers.availability_summary: Teaches adult and teen guitar at West Branch and Astoria on weeknights, plus select online slots.

teachers.bio: Sara is a vocal coach specializing in teen and adult singers who want healthy technique for pop, musical theatre, and classical styles.
teachers.education: MM in Vocal Performance, Juilliard School
teachers.teaching_style: Supportive and technical, with lots of attention to breath, alignment, and vocal health.
teachers.availability_summary: Leads teen group voice classes at East Branch on Tuesdays and Thursdays and offers online lessons for adults.

teachers.bio: Michael teaches adult piano and music theory, helping busy professionals make steady progress with realistic practice plans.
teachers.education: MM in Music Theory, Northwestern University
teachers.teaching_style: Analytical yet friendly, breaking down concepts into clear, manageable steps.
teachers.availability_summary: Teaches adult piano at West Branch on weeknights and offers late-evening online lessons.

teachers.bio: Ana directs several of our teen group voice classes and also teaches beginner guitar. She loves building community in her ensembles.
teachers.education: BA in Vocal Performance, NYU Steinhardt
teachers.teaching_style: Enthusiastic and collaborative, encouraging students to listen to each other and blend.
teachers.availability_summary: Leads teen group voice classes on Tuesday and Thursday evenings at East Branch; limited private slots.

teachers.bio: David is a multi-instrumentalist who teaches both piano and guitar, with a focus on modern styles and improvisation.
teachers.education: BM in Jazz Studies, New School for Jazz and Contemporary Music
teachers.teaching_style: Creative and improvisation-friendly, encouraging students to explore their own musical ideas.
teachers.availability_summary: Teaches piano and guitar at Main Studio on Tuesdays and Fridays, with Wednesday online sessions.

teachers.bio: Linda focuses on young beginners in piano and violin, using play-based methods to keep early lessons engaging.
teachers.education: MM in Music Education, Teachers College, Columbia University
teachers.teaching_style: Gentle and playful, using stories and imagery to teach musical concepts.
teachers.availability_summary: Teaches early-childhood piano and violin at Park Slope and Uptown locations.

teachers.bio: Rachel is an online-focused voice and piano teacher who works primarily with adult beginners and returning musicians.
teachers.education: BM in Music, University of Michigan
teachers.teaching_style: Supportive and structured, with clear practice plans and regular check-ins.
teachers.availability_summary: Offers flexible online voice and piano lessons for adults, including Sunday sessions.

faq_sections.title: Standard cancellation window
faq_sections.content_html:
Our standard cancellation window is 24 hours before your scheduled lesson start time.

  • Cancelling or rescheduling more than 24 hours in advance allows us to release the time to other students and is not subject to any fee.
  • Cancellations received with exactly 24 hours' notice are also treated as on-time and may be eligible for a make-up option depending on teacher availability.
  • Cancellations inside the 24-hour window are treated as late cancellations; see the section below.

To cancel, use your online account, call the front desk, or email us. The timestamp on your request determines whether it is considered on-time or late.

faq_sections.title: Late cancellations (less than 24 hours)
faq_sections.content_html:
A late cancellation is any cancellation made less than 24 hours before your scheduled lesson start time.

  • The lesson is charged in full; tuition for that lesson is not refunded or credited.
  • The lesson is generally not eligible for a complimentary make-up.
  • If the teacher has an opening, you may request a paid make-up lesson. See the "Is there a fee for make-up lessons?" FAQ for current per-lesson fees.
  • Repeated late cancellations may result in losing your reserved weekly time slot.

These policies ensure that teachers are compensated for reserved time and that our schedule remains predictable for all families.

faq_sections.title: How to cancel or reschedule
faq_sections.content_html:
You can cancel or request to reschedule a lesson in one of three ways:

  1. Online: Log in, go to the Schedule tab, select the lesson, and click Cancel or Reschedule.
  2. Phone: Call the studio during business hours and speak with our front desk team.
  3. Email: Send an email with the student name, teacher name, lesson day/time, and your preferred action.

Please note that the time stamp of your online request, call, or email is used to determine whether your cancellation is considered on-time or late.

faq_sections.title: Standard make-up lesson fees
faq_sections.content_html:
Make-up lessons involve additional scheduling outside your regular weekly time. To help compensate teachers for this extra time, we charge the following standard fees:

  • 30-minute make-up lesson: $15 per make-up lesson.
  • 45-minute or 60-minute make-up lesson: $25 per make-up lesson.

These fees are charged to the primary payment method on file when the make-up is confirmed. In rare circumstances (such as a studio-initiated closure), the studio may waive make-up fees at its discretion.

faq_sections.title: When make-up fees apply
faq_sections.content_html:
Make-up lesson fees apply when:

  • You cancelled the original lesson on time (at least 24 hours in advance) and received a make-up credit.
  • You choose to use that credit by booking a specific make-up slot with an available teacher.

Make-up fees do not typically apply if the studio cancels your lesson due to teacher illness or a studio-wide closure; in those cases, we either arrange a complimentary make-up or apply a credit to your account.

faq_sections.title: Steps to schedule a make-up lesson
faq_sections.content_html:
After an eligible cancellation, follow these steps to use your make-up credit:

  1. Open the email titled "Make-Up Lesson Options" or log in and go to the Schedule page.
  2. Filter by your instrument, location, and (optionally) your usual teacher.
  3. Review the list of available make-up slots and select a date and time.
  4. Confirm the displayed make-up fee amount and click Confirm.

Once confirmed, the make-up appears on your schedule and the corresponding fee is processed automatically.

faq_sections.title: Monthly billing cycle
faq_sections.content_html:
Memberships are billed on a monthly recurring basis:

  • Your billing date is the same calendar day each month as your original sign-up date.
  • The monthly charge covers the upcoming block of weekly lessons (typically 4 per month).
  • Any applicable signup fee is charged once at the time you enroll.

If your billing date falls on a weekend or holiday, the charge may process on the next business day while keeping your membership active without interruption.

faq_sections.title: Which lessons offer trial options?
faq_sections.content_html:
Most private lesson offerings for piano, guitar, violin, voice, and drums include a trial option.

  • On the Lessons page, look for offerings with a "Book Trial Lesson" button on their detail page.
  • Group classes (such as semester-long ensembles) may or may not offer trials; when they do, it is clearly listed on the class page.

Trial availability can vary by location and teacher, so we recommend checking the specific lesson you are interested in or contacting our front desk for the latest information.

events.title: Spring 2025 Beginner Piano Recital  Main Studio (Under 10)
events.description: A friendly, low-pressure recital for beginner piano students under age 10 at our Main Studio. Each student performs one short piece to celebrate their progress with family and friends.
events.eligibility_summary: Current beginner piano students under age 10 enrolled at any studio location.

events.title: Spring 2025 Beginner Piano Recital  East Branch (Under 10)
events.description: A free recital for young beginner piano students under age 10 at the East Branch Studio, featuring short solo pieces and group photos.
events.eligibility_summary: Beginner piano students under 10 taking lessons at East Branch or nearby studios.

events.title: Spring 2025 Beginner Piano Recital  Brooklyn Heights (Under 10)
events.description: A neighborhood-focused recital for beginner piano students under age 10 at our Brooklyn Heights Studio. Perfect as a first performance experience.
events.eligibility_summary: Current beginner-level piano students age 59 at the Brooklyn Heights and Park Slope studios.

events.title: Spring 2025 Intermediate Piano Recital  Main Studio (Ages 712)
events.description: A recital for intermediate piano students ages 712 at the Main Studio, featuring solo pieces and occasional duets.
events.eligibility_summary: Intermediate piano students ages 712 by teacher recommendation.

events.title: Teen Voice Showcase  East Branch Studio
events.description: An end-of-semester showcase for teen group voice students at the East Branch Studio, featuring solo and ensemble numbers.
events.eligibility_summary: Open to students enrolled in teen voice classes or private voice lessons at East Branch.

events.title: Piano Practice Power-Up: 20 Minutes a Day
events.description: A 90-minute interactive workshop for parents and students on designing effective 20-minute daily piano practice routines, led by our piano faculty.
events.eligibility_summary: Recommended for piano students ages 7+ and their parents; open to the public.

events.title: Violin Masterclass with Guest Artist at Main Studio
events.description: An advanced violin masterclass featuring a guest artist, with selected students performing and observers welcome.
events.eligibility_summary: Performers by teacher nomination; observers welcome with registration.

events.title: Summer 2025 Rock Band Camp  Main Studio
events.description: A one-week daytime camp where teens form bands, rehearse with coaches, and finish with a performance for family and friends.
events.eligibility_summary: Teen instrumentalists and vocalists ages 1317 with at least 1 year of experience on their instrument.

events.title: Winter 2024 Mixed-Instrument Recital  Main Studio
events.description: A mixed recital featuring piano, guitar, violin, voice, and drum students of all ages and levels at the Main Studio.
events.eligibility_summary: Open to current students of any instrument by teacher recommendation.

events.title: Online Workshop: Healthy Vocal Technique for Adult Singers
events.description: A live online workshop covering breath support, warm-ups, and sustainable technique for adult voice students.
events.eligibility_summary: Adult singers of any style; no prior experience required.

events.title: Summer 2025 Teen Drums & Rhythm Recital  Main Studio
events.description: A rhythmic recital featuring teen drum students and small rhythm ensembles performing groove-based pieces.
events.eligibility_summary: Teen drum students at intermediate level or above by teacher recommendation.

events.title: Spring 2025 Kids Piano Recital  Park Slope Studio
events.description: A cozy recital for kids piano students at the Park Slope Studio, with a mix of solo and duet performances.
events.eligibility_summary: Piano students ages 712 currently studying at Park Slope or Brooklyn Heights.
